title: Listen and Watch the Quran: Complete Audio Recitations and Video Collection
excerpt: The Quran is Al-Qur'an — The Recitation. Listen to any Surah recited by the world's greatest reciters, or watch complete Quranic recitation videos. The most complete free Quran audio and video resource online.
author: QuranBookk
content:
## The Quran Was Never Meant to Be Read Silently

The name of this Book is القُرْآن — *Al-Qur'an*. The word is derived from *qara'a* (قرأ), meaning *to recite*, *to read aloud*, *to proclaim*. It does not mean *the text* or *the writing* or *the scripture*. It means, precisely and literally, **The Recitation**.

This is not a minor linguistic point. It is a foundational truth about the nature of the Quran. The Quran was transmitted orally from the Angel Jibril to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ — not as a written text but as a recitation, with specific sounds, pronunciation rules, and melodic dimensions. It was then transmitted from the Prophet ﷺ to his Companions through the same oral channel. The Quran as sound came centuries before the Quran as written text became widespread.

When you listen to the Quran recited, you are experiencing it in its original, intended form.

The [QuranBookk Quran Audio](/quran/audio/) and [Quran Video](/quran/video/) collections bring that experience to any browser, on any device, completely free.

## The Science Behind How Recitation Affects You

Every Muslim who has listened to a great recitation knows the experience: something shifts. The breath slows. The mind quiets. The words land differently when heard than when read.

This is not mystical — it is neurological. Human auditory processing routes directly through the limbic system (the brain's emotional center) before reaching the prefrontal cortex. Sound, in other words, reaches the emotional layer of consciousness before the analytical layer. This is why music moves people to tears before they understand the lyrics. It is why the Quran recited aloud has always been more immediately moving than the Quran read silently.

The Tajweed rules — the precise phonetic system governing Quranic recitation — were developed over centuries specifically to preserve the exact sounds of the revelation as transmitted from the Prophet ﷺ. Every lengthened vowel, every nasalization, every point of articulation is standardized. When you hear a correctly recited Quran, you are hearing sounds that have been transmitted in an unbroken chain from the Prophet ﷺ to the present day.

## The World's Greatest Reciters — In One Collection

The [Quran Audio collection](/quran/audio/) features recitations by the most renowned Quranic reciters in the world:

**Sheikh Abdul Basit Abdus-Samad** — Widely considered the greatest Quran reciter of the 20th century. His *Mujawwad* (melodically embellished) style is the gold standard of Quranic recitation. His Surah Ar-Rahman is among the most listened-to Quran recordings in history.

**Sheikh Mishary Rashid Al-Afasy** — The most-followed contemporary Quran reciter globally, with hundreds of millions of online listeners. His voice is the one most young Muslims today recognise as the sound of the Quran. His recitation style balances clarity with beauty.

**Sheikh Maher Al-Muaiqly** — The Imam of the Grand Mosque in Makkah, whose Tarawih recitations during Ramadan are broadcast worldwide. His clear, measured style is one of the most widely used for memorisation.

**Sheikh Abdul Rahman As-Sudais** — Former Imam of the Grand Mosque in Makkah. His powerful voice and emotional delivery have made his recitations among the most emotionally moving Quran recordings available.

**Sheikh Saad Al-Ghamdi** — Known for a clear, accessible recitation style ideal for following along with the text. Widely used for Tajweed learning and Khatm recitation.

**Sheikh Muhammad Siddiq Al-Minshawi** — A classical Egyptian reciter of extraordinary technical precision, whose style is a reference point for traditional Quranic recitation.

## How to Use the Audio Collection

For each Surah in the [Quran Audio feature](/quran/audio/):
- Select any reciter from the collection
- Listen to the full Surah or navigate verse by verse
- Use audio alongside the [Quran Surahs feature](/quran/surahs/) for simultaneous reading and listening
- For the full Mushaf reading experience with audio, combine the [Digital Quran](/digital-quran) with audio playing in the same browser tab

<Callout type="tip">
For Tajweed improvement: Listen to the same Surah recited by two different reciters — one Mujawwad (melodically full, like Abdul Basit) and one Murattal (measured and clear, like Al-Ghamdi). The Murattal style is easier to follow for pronunciation learning; the Mujawwad style demonstrates the full sonic beauty of the recitation.
</Callout>

## Quran Videos: Watch as Well as Listen

The [Quran Video collection](/quran/video/) provides the complete visual recitation experience — Quranic recitations with the Arabic text displayed on screen, typically in a beautifully designed format that allows you to follow the Arabic as you listen.

Video recitation is particularly valuable for:
- Following along with the Arabic text while listening
- Sharing Quranic content in gatherings or on screens
- Combining auditory and visual input for stronger memorisation reinforcement
- Accessible viewing on televisions and large screens via browser casting

## The Commute Khatm: Completing the Quran by Listening

At a moderate recitation pace, the complete Quran takes approximately 8–10 hours to recite. A Muslim commuting 45 minutes each way, 5 days a week, accumulates 7.5 hours of listening time weekly. In just two weeks of commutes, the full Quran can be completed by audio — a Khatm that requires no additional time from a busy schedule.

During Ramadan, combining audio listening (during commutes, household tasks, exercise) with the reading schedule from [Quran Juzs](/quran/juzs/) makes a double Khatm — one through reading, one through listening — genuinely achievable.

## The Quran as Sound — Free, Any Device, Any Browser

The [Quran Audio collection](/quran/audio/) and [Quran Video collection](/quran/video/) are free, require no account, and work from any browser on any device. The world's greatest reciters. Every Surah. One platform.
